fresh_facts.claim: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 19, 2026, in retaliation against a US naval blockade on Iranian ports.
Iranian IRGC-linked gunboats fired on at least two Indian-flagged oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, 2026.
The US maintains a naval blockade on Iranian ports and has turned back at least 23 ships, with plans to board Iran-linked tankers in international waters.
President Trump stated that US-Iran talks are progressing but emphasized no concessions will be made, with no ceasefire lifting of the blockade until a peace deal is reached.
Iran's new supreme leader threatened 'bitter defeats' for the US and Israel via state TV on April 19, 2026.
Iranian officials warned of halting 15 million barrels of daily oil production for a year in response to the US blockade.
The IRGC reportedly controls up to 50% of Iran's oil exports, which are now targeted by the US blockade to squeeze regime funding.
A ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire on Wednesday (April 23, 2026), with Pakistan mediating new talks.
Thousands marched in Tehran urging the regime not to compromise with the US amid economic strain from war and blockades.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei appointed Major General Amir Hatami as overall Artesh (regular military) commander, replacing Major General Abdol Rahim Mousavi.
Israeli airstrikes killed IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Brigadier General Mohammad Kazemi and his deputy Hassan Mohaghegh, targeting regime intelligence capabilities.
An Iranian Armed Forces General Staff outlet called for Basij paramilitary nationwide deployment for patrols and countering alleged Mossad infiltration to suppress dissent.
Iran's economy has sharply deteriorated 100 days after a January 2026 crackdown, with high inflation, unemployment, and war impacts.
Israel expanded strikes on June 14, 2026, to critical energy infrastructure including the Shahran oil depot near Tehran, degrading Iran's military and nuclear sustainment capacity.
IDF strikes hit internal security sites including the Law Enforcement Command headquarters and the Ministries of Justice, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence.
Iran fired two ballistic missile waves at Israel during the conflict, according to reports.
US envoys arrived in Islamabad, Pakistan on Monday to conduct talks with Tehran regarding ceasefire negotiations.
Iran is considering a temporary halt to shipments through the Hormuz Strait to facilitate a second round of US-Iran ceasefire talks.
Iranian officials stated they will not return to US-Iran nuclear negotiations until Israel halts attacks and Iran completes retaliation.
The Turkish Foreign Minister expressed optimism for extending the US-Iran ceasefire.
Israel reported over 150 Hezbollah operatives killed pre-ceasefire and expanded its IDF air campaign to regime institutions and airbases.
The US arrested an Iranian national for Basij-linked visa fraud.
Energy shortages in Iran, alongside degradation of the coercive security apparatus from Israeli strikes, risk further destabilization of the regime.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces converging existential pressures: a collapsing April 23 ceasefire deadline with US blockade of 23+ vessels, comprehensive Israeli strikes on June 14-15 eliminating IRGC leadership and energy infrastructure, and cascading internal instability marked by mandatory Basij deployment, succession upheaval, and economic freefall amid threatened 15 million barrel production halts.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 14-15 killed IRGC Intelligence Chief Mohammad Kazemi and deputies while destroying critical energy infrastructure including Shahran oil depot, regime ministries, and airbases, while simultaneously degrading coercive security apparatus capacity and forcing emergency Basij nationwide deployment to suppress dissent.
